title: Longest Subarray With At Most K Distinct Integers
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the length of the longest contiguous subarray that contains at most k distinct integers.
You must solve this using an efficient sliding window approach (O(n) time).

examples.explanation: The longest subarray with at most 2 distinct integers is [1,2,1,2] with length 4.
The longest valid window is [1,2,1,3] with 3 distinct integers. Length = 4.
All elements are identical, so the entire array is valid. Length = 4.

edge_cases: k = 1 (window must contain identical values only)
All elements identical (entire array is valid)
k >= number of distinct elements in nums (entire array is valid)
Strictly increasing array with small k
notes: Use a hashmap to track frequencies of elements in the current window.
Expand the right pointer; shrink the left pointer when distinct count exceeds k.
The answer is the maximum window size observed while maintaining validity.
hints: Hint 1: Maintain a dictionary mapping value → count inside the window.
Hint 2: When the dictionary has more than k keys, shrink from the left.
Hint 3: Remove keys from the dictionary when their count drops to zero.
Hint 4: Track the best window length as you expand the right pointer.
